---
title: "R Workbook: Novice to Social Scientist"
---

:::{.phase-header}
# R Workbook

**A hands-on guide to R for content analysis research**

Seven chapters take you from building a codebook to publishing a professional research portfolio --- with narrated video walkthroughs for every chapter.
:::

---

## What You'll Learn

This workbook is your companion for the R programming portion of the course (Phases III--V). Each chapter builds on the previous one, following the same dataset through the full research pipeline.

| Chapter | Title | What You'll Do |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| 1 | [Codebook](01-codebook.qmd) | Turn qualitative observations into numeric codes |
| 2 | [Data Collection](02-data-collection.qmd) | Code your full sample with consistency checks |
| 3 | [Meet R](03-meet-r.qmd) | Set up R, RStudio, and your project environment |
| 4 | [Data Wrangling](04-data-wrangling.qmd) | Import, clean, and export your dataset |
| 5 | [Describing Data](05-describing-data.qmd) | Build frequency tables and publication-ready charts |
| 6 | [Inferencing Data](06-inferencing-data.qmd) | Run chi-square tests and interpret results |
| 7 | [Publishing](07-publishing.qmd) | Assemble your portfolio as a Quarto Book |

---

## How to Use This Workbook

:::{.callout-tip}
## Watch, Then Do

Every chapter opens with a **narrated video walkthrough**. Watch it first to get the big picture, then work through the written content at your own pace. The video and text cover the same material --- use whichever format helps you learn.
:::

1. **Watch the video** at the top of each chapter
2. **Follow along in RStudio** --- the code blocks are designed to run in order
3. **Use the class dataset** (`music_data_raw.csv`) for practice
4. **Apply to your own data** for the final portfolio

---

## Reference

- [R & RStudio Quick Reference](appendix-a-reference.qmd) --- common functions and syntax
- [Glossary](appendix-b-glossary.qmd) --- key terms defined

---

:::{.callout-info}
## Need the Workbook Offline?

The workbook is also available as a standalone Quarto Book. Download it from the [Resources](../resources/index.qmd) page or install `coursepackR` to access the project files directly in RStudio.
:::
